name: Qadi Shurayh
title: Court Judge
shortDescription: The judge of Kufa who, through his fatwa or meaningful silence, religiously justified the killing of Imam Hussain (AS) for the ignorant Kufan army.
fullStory:
Qadi Shurayh was a veteran judge who had held the position of Kufa's judge since the time of the second caliph. He symbolizes court scholars and those who sell their religion, putting faith at the service of power.

When Hani ibn Urwa was being tortured in Ibn Ziyad's palace, his tribe (Madhhij) besieged the palace. The frightened Ibn Ziyad asked Qadi Shurayh to go out and tell the people that Hani is alive and well. Shurayh lied, dispersed the crowd, and caused Hani's martyrdom.

But his main role in the Karbala tragedy was when he issued a fatwa (or tacit approval) declaring Imam Hussain's (AS) rising against the caliph of the time (Yazid) as 'rebellion against the Imam of the time', rendering his blood permissible to shed.

This fatwa from the city's judge gave a religious pretext to the uninsightful Kufan army to comfortably go to war against the Prophet's grandson. Shurayh lived in comfort until the end of his life, but his name went down in history as the symbol of scholars' silence and betrayal.
quoteArabic: إِنَّ الحُسَيْنَ قَدْ خَرَجَ عَنْ حَدِّ الدِّينِ... فَوَجَبَ قَتْلُهُ...
quote: (In the fatwa attributed to him:) Hussain has stepped out of the boundaries of religion and rebelled against the caliph of the time... thus his killing is obligatory.
